skill.name: ai-avatar-video
skill.description: Create AI avatar, talking-head, and lip-sync videos on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI. Routes across ByteDance OmniHuman, Wan-AI Wan 2-7, HappyHorse 1.0, and Seedance v2 Pro models. Picks the right model for user intent: UGC voiceover, virtual presenter, dubbed product demo, lip-synced character, or dialog scene.
skill.summary: Routes user requests to the correct RunComfy audio-driven avatar model and generates the runcomfy run command.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All 115 static findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. Backtick pattern matches hit Markdown inline code formatting, not Ruby shell execution. Network URL matches are legitimate documentation links to runcomfy.com and placeholder example URLs. Filesystem matches reference a documentation description of token storage location. Weak crypto and system reconnaissance findings are scanner hallucinations on normal documentation text. The skill is a legitimate CLI wrapper for the runcomfy tool with proper tool scoping (Bash(runcomfy *) ) and includes a thorough Security and Privacy section.

low_findings.title: Markdown backtick formatting misidentified as Ruby shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 67 instances of backtick characters as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All instances are standard Markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `runcomfy run`) or Markdown code fences. No Ruby shell execution is present. The skill is written in Markdown, not Ruby. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs are legitimate documentation and example placeholders
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 36 URLs as hardcoded network references. All URLs point to runcomfy.com (the service the skill wraps), skills.sh (the skill marketplace), docs.runcomfy.com (documentation), or your-cdn.example (placeholder URL in example code). No data exfiltration or malicious endpoints detected. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Hidden file reference is documentation of token storage, not active filesystem access
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:286 for hidden file access. Line 286 reads: runcomfy login writes the API token to ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. This is a transparency notice describing where tokens are stored, not active filesystem access. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm finding is a scanner hallucination
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 5 instances of weak cryptographic algorithms at lines 5, 76, 135, 200, 265. These lines contain no cryptographic references. Line 5 is YAML frontmatter, line 76 is model description text, line 135 is prompt guidance, line 200 is documentation about audio tone, and line 265 is a section header for exit codes. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance finding is a scanner hallucination from Avoid for: documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 5 instances of system reconnaissance at lines 63, 68, 73, 78, 83. These lines contain Avoid for: documentation tips (e.g., Avoid for: no audio file available, Avoid for: precise lip-sync). These are model selection guidance notes, not system reconnaissance. FALSE POSITIVE.

content.user_title: Create AI avatar and talking head videos with RunComfy
content.value_statement: Creating AI avatar videos often requires navigating complex model selection across multiple providers. This skill automatically routes your request to the correct RunComfy audio-driven model and generates the exact command needed.
actual_capabilities: Generate avatar videos from a portrait image and audio file using OmniHuman
Create lip-synced videos with full scene control using Wan 2-7 audio_url
Produce talking head videos from a written script using HappyHorse in-pass audio
Compose cinematic multi-modal shots with Seedance v2 Pro reference images and audio
Animate stylized characters with audio synchronization using Wan 2-2 Animate
Generate the exact runcomfy run command with the correct model endpoint and JSON input
limitations: Requires the runcomfy CLI to be installed and the user to be signed in
Cannot modify or edit generated videos after creation; regeneration is needed
Video quality depends on the quality of input portrait and audio files
Some models require an audio file URL; text-to-speech generation is not included

use_cases.title: UGC product video with voiceover
use_cases.description: A content creator needs a quick product demo video. They upload a portrait and a voiceover MP3, and the skill routes to OmniHuman for a natural talking head video.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media marketers

use_cases.title: Multi-language brand video from one portrait
use_cases.description: A marketing team wants the same presenter to speak in multiple languages. They use the same portrait with different audio files per language to create dubbed clips.

use_cases.title: Script-to-video without audio recording
use_cases.description: A product manager has a written script but no audio recording. The skill routes to HappyHorse which generates speech from the script text directly.

prompt_templates.title: Basic talking head from portrait and audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking head video from this portrait image and voiceover audio. Use the OmniHuman model.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a portrait image and a pre-recorded audio file

prompt_templates.title: Video from a written script only
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a talking head video but I don't have an audio file. Here is the script: [insert script]. Make the speaker look at the camera and deliver this naturally.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a script but no audio recording

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic monologue with reference images
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cinematic video with this person as the subject. Use this reference image and this audio track. The scene should feel like a documentary interview with soft lighting.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a polished cinematic shot with subject and audio reference

prompt_templates.title: Animated character lip sync
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an illustrated character and an audio file. Make the character speak and move naturally to this audio.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a stylized character image (not photoreal) with audio

output_examples.input: Create a talking head video from this presenter portrait and my product demo voiceover.
output_examples.output: The skill selects the OmniHuman model, constructs a runcomfy run command with the provided image_url and audio_url, and generates a video where the presenter speaks naturally to the voiceover.

output_examples.input: Make this character say Welcome to our channel in a friendly tone. Here is the character image and the audio file.
output_examples.output: The skill routes to Wan 2-2 Animate for stylized character animation, builds the command with the character image and audio, and generates a full-body animated video with synchronized mouth movement.

best_practices: Use high-quality portrait images with good lighting and clear facial features for best results
Provide clean audio without background music for the most accurate lip sync
Match the emotional tone of the audio to the prompt description for natural-looking expressions
anti_patterns: Do not use HappyHorse when precise lip sync to a specific audio file is required; audio is regenerated each call
Do not use Seedance v2 Pro for simple portrait plus audio jobs; it is slower and overpowered for basic use
Do not include spoken words in the Wan 2-7 prompt; the model syncs to the audio waveform, not the prompt text

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need the runcomfy CLI installed and a RunComfy account. Install with npm i -g @runcomfy/cli and sign in with runcomfy login.

faq.question: Which model should I use for a simple talking head video?
faq.answer: OmniHuman is the default and best choice for most talking head use cases. It takes one portrait and one audio file and produces natural results.

faq.question: Can I create a video without an audio file?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the HappyHorse model which generates speech from the text script you provide inside the prompt.

faq.question: What audio formats are supported?
faq.answer: Audio files should be provided as URLs. Common formats like MP3 and WAV work with RunComfy models. Check the model documentation for specific format requirements.

faq.question: Can I use this for commercial projects?
faq.answer: You must ensure you have the rights to both the portrait image and the audio file. Respect likeness and voice consent rights. The skill is MIT licensed.

faq.question: What happens if the generation fails?
faq.answer: The runcomfy CLI returns exit codes to indicate failure reasons. Common issues include bad input JSON, authentication errors, and upstream service errors.
